text:
Poland’s Aleksandra Miroslaw managed to break her speed climbing record twice within just 16 minutes at theParisOlympics.

The Olympic sport climbing events got underway on Monday having first been introduced to the games in Tokyo three years ago.

Two-time world champion Miroslaw is one of the favourites in the women’s speed event and held both the world and Olympic record going into Paris.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

And her rivals will be fearing her even more after the Pole broke her record not once but twice during the qualification heats.

In her first run, the 30-year-old blitzed up the climbing wall in a time of 6.21 seconds – 0.03 seconds quicker than the previous world record.

As it turns out, Miroslaw was just warming up, as just 16 minutes later she set a staggering time of 6.06 seconds during her second climb.

That obviously saw her seeded first for the elimination heats where she easily beat South Africa’s Aniya Holding with a (relatively sluggish) time of 6.10 seconds.

She’ll now take on Spain’s Leslie Romero in Wednesday’s quarter-final, with the semi-finals and final following shortly after that.

Though she set the inaugural Olympic record in Tokyo, Miroslaw left Japan without a medal after finishing fourth, with Janja Garnbret of Slovenia winning gold.

There’s British interest in the climbing too, with Toby Roberts and Hamish McArthur through to the semi-finals of the men’s boulder and lead event, with the former expected to be in medal contention.

title: Olympian breaks her own world record twice within a few minutes | Metro News
description: Poland's Aleksandra Miroslaw managed to break her speed climbing record twice within just 16 minutes at the Paris 2024 Olympics.
